text_blocks.text: A monitor is another kind of high-level synchronization primitive

runs.text: is another kind of high-level synchronization primitive

runs.text: One monitor has multiple entry points

text_blocks.text: Think multiple methods in a Java class

text_blocks.text: Only one process may be in the monitor at any time

runs.text: Only one process may be in the monitor at any time

text_blocks.text: Enforces mutual exclusion - less chance for programming errors

runs.text: Enforces mutual exclusion - less chance for programming errors

text_blocks.text: Variables belonging to monitor are protected from simultaneous access

runs.text: Variables belonging to monitor are protected from simultaneous access

text_blocks.text: Procedures in monitor are guaranteed to have mutual exclusion

runs.text: Procedures in monitor are guaranteed to have mutual exclusion

text_blocks.text: Only one process can be executing in either produce or consume at any time

runs.text: Only one process can be executing in either produce or consume at any time

images.ai_description: Code snippet of a Java class `SharedBuffer<T>` with synchronized keyword, constructors, and methods `produce` and `consume`, demonstrating basic concurrency handling structure.
Multi-threaded producer-consumer code in C++: `SharedBuffer sb` used. `P0` produces items with `sb.produce(item)`. `P1` consumes items using `sb.consume()`.
